name: Magnolia DXP and Headless CMS API
what_it_does: Enterprise headless CMS and DXP with REST and GraphQL API — enabling organizations to create visual pages from headless content blocks, manage multi-site and multi-language content, personalize experiences, run A/B tests, and integrate with third-party DXP components (commerce, search, analytics) via a composable architecture, with Java-based on-premises or Magnolia Cloud hosting.
use_cases: Delivering headless content via Magnolia REST Content API for Next.js or React frontends
Managing multi-site, multi-language content via Magnolia Management API for content operations
Integrating Magnolia with commerce platforms (Salesforce B2C, SAP Commerce) via API for unified commerce content
Personalizing experiences and running A/B tests via Magnolia Personalization and Targeting API
Orchestrating composable DXP components (search, PIM, CDP) via Magnolia's integration layer
not_for: Small teams needing rapid CMS deployment (Magnolia has significant setup complexity)
Organizations without Java development capability for on-premises
Simple headless CMS without multi-site or personalization requirements
best_when: A European enterprise with multi-site, multi-language requirements needs a composable DXP that can integrate with existing commerce and marketing technology — especially manufacturers, retailers, and financial services companies in German-speaking markets.
avoid_when: You're small/mid-market, need rapid deployment without Java expertise, don't require multi-site management, or prefer US-headquartered enterprise software.

auth.notes: API key for REST delivery endpoint. OAuth2 for Management API. Bearer token auth. Workspace-scoped with role-based permissions (Magnolia security framework). Webhook for content publish events. REST Content API for headless delivery. Management API for content creation and workflow. Java SDK for module development. JavaScript frontend SDK available. SAML SSO via Magnolia SSO module.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Community Edition (open source, limited features); no free SaaS tier
pricing.paid_tiers: Standard from €50,000/year; Enterprise from €150,000/year; Cloud custom; contact sales
pricing.notes: Swiss company (Basel). Founded 1997 as InfoFabrik. Magnolia since 2003. Privately held. 300+ employees. 350+ enterprise customers. Gartner Magic Quadrant recognized for DXP. Customers include Generali, Lufthansa Technik, Swisscom, Deutsche Telekom, DHL. Strong in German-speaking markets (DACH: Germany, Austria, Switzerland). Java-based architecture on Apache Jackrabbit (JCR) content repository. Community Edition (open source) + Enterprise Edition (commercial). Composable DXP strategy. Deep e-commerce integration expertise (SAP Commerce, Salesforce B2C, Commercetools).

performance.rate_limits: Enterprise rate limits per contract; CDN-delivered content at edge speed

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST Content API + Management API. API key + OAuth2 auth. Good enterprise documentation (primarily English, some German). Webhook for content events. Java-based — Java developer required for on-premises customization. SAML SSO for enterprise identity. Swiss company with GDPR-first engineering. No GraphQL — REST only for headless delivery. Community Edition for evaluation.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Content reads fully idempotent. Node reads idempotent by path or UUID. Management API mutations use UUID for idempotency. Webhook delivery idempotent by event ID.
known_agent_gotchas: Java developer required for on-premises modules and customization
Community Edition for evaluation — Enterprise features (personalization, A/B testing) require paid license
No GraphQL — REST delivery API only for headless (GraphQL on roadmap)
DACH market focus — English documentation may lag German community resources
Multi-site/multi-language configuration requires significant Magnolia expertise

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR, ISO27001, SOC2 compliant. EU, Swiss, US data residency. API key + OAuth2 auth. Swiss company with strict privacy standards (Swiss data protection law). SAML SSO for enterprise identity. Content may include personalization profiles — GDPR compliance required. Open-source Community Edition allows security audit. Enterprise Edition adds additional security controls. Swiss and German-market focus implies high regulatory compliance standards.
